prompts.title: Evocative Menu Descriptions for North Indian Mains
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior menu copywriter with 12 years of experience writing for Indian fine-dining and casual chains like Punjab Grill and Saffron. I need you to write evocative one-line menu descriptions (18-25 words each) for 12 North Indian main courses I will paste below. Here is the context: I run a 60-cover Punjabi restaurant in Powai, Mumbai with average ticket size ₹650. My target diner is the 28-45 year old urban professional who appreciates authenticity but reads menus on Zomato before visiting. Please provide: (1) the dish name in regular case, (2) a sensory description that names hero ingredients, cooking method, and a regional/historical hook, (3) allergen tags (gluten/dairy/nut), (4) spice indicator (mild/medium/spicy). Format as a markdown table. Constraints: avoid clichés like 'mouth-watering', 'authentic'; do not invent ingredients I have not listed; keep it Hinglish-free (English only); ensure FSSAI labelling readiness. Dishes I will paste: [LIST 12 DISH NAMES WITH KEY INGREDIENTS].

prompts.title: Plate Cost and Margin Calculator for New Dish
prompts.prompt: Act as a chef-owner with 10 years of restaurant cost-control experience in Bengaluru, Pune, and Hyderabad. I need you to compute the plate cost, target menu price, and margin for a new dish I am adding to my menu. Here is the context: I run a 4-outlet South Indian QSR brand. I want a 28-32% food cost ratio at our average ticket size ₹220. The dish is a Mysore Masala Dosa Combo (1 dosa, 2 chutneys, 1 sambar, 1 filter coffee). I will paste my recipe (per portion grams + INR price per kg from my supplier in Yeshwanthpur mandi). Please provide: (1) ingredient-wise raw material cost in INR (rounded to 2 decimals), (2) total raw cost per plate, (3) packaging cost (₹6 estimate for cloud kitchen orders), (4) suggested menu price for both dine-in and Swiggy/Zomato (with 20% commission absorbed), (5) blended food cost % at each price, (6) break-even units per day. Format as a single combined table. Constraints: factor in 4% raw material wastage; price round to nearest ₹5; margins must clear 30% on aggregator orders.

prompts.title: Kitchen Hygiene SOP Aligned with FSSAI
prompts.prompt: Act as a HACCP-certified consultant with 14 years of advising Indian restaurants on FSSAI compliance. Draft a daily kitchen hygiene SOP for my 1,200 sqft cloud kitchen in Gurgaon serving 400 orders/day across two cuisines (Pan-Asian and Biryani). Cover: (1) opening checklist (chef on duty, temperature checks of walk-in chiller and freezer, surface sanitation), (2) personal hygiene (uniform, hand-wash frequency, no jewellery, beard nets), (3) raw material receiving (FSSAI-licensed vendor only, supplier invoice match, visual + temperature check), (4) cross-contamination controls (colour-coded chopping boards + knives, separate veg/non-veg prep zones), (5) cleaning rotation (3-bucket mop, 6-hour sanitation cycle), (6) closing checklist (waste segregation per Solid Waste Mgmt Rules 2016, pest log). Format as a printable A4 checklist with check-boxes and a sign-off row. Constraints: align with FSSAI Schedule 4 Part II for restaurants; mention CCP records to maintain for 6 months; use Hindi-friendly icons for line staff.

prompts.title: Service Standards Hindi-English Staff Manual
prompts.prompt: Act as a hotel F&B trainer with 15 years at Taj and Marriott India. Write a 1-page bilingual (English + simple Hindi) service standards module for line waiters at my 80-cover restaurant in Connaught Place, Delhi. Cover the 12-step service flow from greeting to settlement, including: greeting + escort to table, water + napkin placement, menu presentation, taking the order with up-sell prompt, confirming spice and allergen, KOT punch, course-pacing, hot vs cold serve order, plate clearing rules, dessert + coffee suggestion, billing presentation, farewell. Provide for each step (1) English instruction (one line), (2) Hindi simple-script equivalent, (3) common mistake to avoid, (4) one verbatim phrase the waiter should say. Format as a 4-column table. Constraints: avoid pure Hindi translation — use Hinglish phrases the waiter actually speaks; pacing tied to a 90-minute average dining slot; align with Indian guest expectations on hospitality (water always full, frequent check-back).

prompts.title: Zomato 1-Star Review Recovery Response
prompts.prompt: Act as a guest-experience manager with 8 years handling Indian aggregator reviews for restaurant groups. I will paste a 1-star Zomato review where the guest complains about (a) cold biryani, (b) wrong order item, and (c) rude delivery exec. Write a public response (under 90 words) and a private follow-up message (under 150 words) per Zomato's response policy. Public response should: own the issue without making excuses, offer a specific recovery, share a resolution channel (DM or branch number), avoid generic templates. Private message should: address each complaint by name, offer a free replacement OR ₹400 voucher (mention which based on issue severity), set 24-hour SLA, sign with the manager's name + designation. Format as two distinct text blocks labelled PUBLIC and PRIVATE. Constraints: tone must be warm Indian English, not corporate; do not blame the rider or kitchen publicly; mention FSSAI-grade complaint handling internally only.

prompts.title: Monsoon Menu Planning for Indian Restaurant
prompts.prompt: Act as an executive chef with 18 years of menu engineering across Indian climate zones. Plan a 14-item monsoon special menu for my 120-cover all-day dining restaurant in Bandra, Mumbai (June-September). Include: (1) 4 hot starters featuring monsoon-friendly ingredients (corn, ginger, garlic, kala chana, makhana), (2) 4 mains with light gravies suited to humid weather, (3) 3 monsoon beverages (kadak chai, masala milk, kashayam), (4) 3 desserts using seasonal mango (Alphonso end June, Banganapalli through July). For each dish provide name, 12-word description, food cost band (₹ category), suggested menu price, plate weight, and a one-line up-sell hook the waiter says. Format as a printable menu sheet plus a costing summary. Constraints: avoid leafy greens (monsoon contamination risk per FSSAI advisory); ensure minimum 3 vegan and 4 jain options; signage must say 'monsoon special' to drive impulse orders.

prompts.title: Cloud Kitchen Brand Launch Naming Pack
prompts.prompt: Act as a brand strategist with 9 years of naming Indian D2C and cloud kitchen brands. I am launching a Mughlai cloud kitchen targeting Gurgaon DLF Phase 1-3 with average ticket ₹450. Provide 12 brand name options across 3 buckets: (1) classical-evocative (Persian/Urdu poetic feel), (2) modern-Hinglish (e.g., 'Daawat & Co.'), (3) descriptive-functional. For each name give the 4-word tagline, a one-line backstory I can put in the Swiggy/Zomato 'about' section, social handle availability check (@handle.in), .in domain availability suggestion, and a trademark search class hint (Class 43). Format as a comparison table with a final recommendation column ranking top 3. Constraints: avoid names already used by Faasos/Behrouz/EatFit; pronounceable in both English and Hindi; under 12 characters for the logo; leave headroom for a multi-brand kitchen company name later.

prompts.title: Diwali Festive Menu Profitability Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a restaurant ops consultant with 11 years of festival menu planning. Help me design and price-analyze a Diwali festive set menu for my multi-cuisine restaurant in Indiranagar, Bengaluru. Target: 250 diners across 5 days (Dhanteras to Bhai Dooj), average pax/table 4. Build: (1) a 7-course Diwali thali (welcome drink + 2 starters + 3 mains + dessert + paan) with Indian regional rotation each day; (2) per-thali plate cost, suggested menu price (₹/person), and projected gross margin; (3) festival-specific ops add-ons (diya placement, marigold rangoli, Lakshmi puja thali for staff); (4) a 3-tier pricing strategy (regular, premium with mocktail, ultra-premium with valet + photography); (5) projected revenue scenarios (low/base/high occupancy). Format as a costed menu doc + financial table. Constraints: factor 35% food cost; assume 18% GST; set premium tier 1.6x regular; staff bonus ₹500/head from premium tier upside.

prompts.title: Recipe Standardization for Multi-Outlet Chain
prompts.prompt: Act as a corporate chef with 14 years of standardizing recipes across multi-outlet Indian QSR brands (similar to Wow Momo, Burger Singh). I need a recipe standardization template for my 8-outlet biryani brand expanding to 20 outlets across South India. Cover for one signature dish (Chicken Hyderabadi Biryani, 350g portion): (1) standard yield (kg + portions), (2) ingredient table with grams + supplier-mandi grade + brand specification, (3) prep steps with critical control points, (4) cooking SOP with time + temp + visual cues, (5) plating standard with photo brief, (6) tolerance ranges (e.g., chicken 90-100g, raita 35g ±2g), (7) shelf-life of pre-cooked components, (8) failure modes + corrective actions. Format as a 2-page recipe card. Constraints: write so a kitchen helper with class-12 education can execute; weights in metric; allow Hindi/Telugu translation column for outlet teams.

prompts.title: Dish Photography Brief for Aggregator Listings
prompts.prompt: Act as a food photographer with 7 years of shooting for Swiggy, Zomato, and Indian D2C food brands. Write a photography brief for 30 dishes I am relaunching on aggregators next month. Brief should include: (1) overall mood (rustic vs studio vs lifestyle), (2) per-dish shot list (hero, top-down, lifestyle, prop crop), (3) crockery palette (avoid white-only — suggest 3 plate types from Mumbai's Wadala wholesale market), (4) lighting setup (natural window or strobe softbox; CRI >95), (5) garnish discipline (real ingredients only, no glycerine spritz), (6) post-production rules (no rainbow saturation; max +12 vibrance), (7) file delivery (RAW + JPEG + 1500px web crop), (8) shoot day schedule (slot kitchen prep with camera setup), (9) shoot cost line items (camera, light, stylist, props, INR estimates). Format as a brief PDF outline. Constraints: must comply with ASCI on misleading visuals; portion sizes shown must equal actual served; budget cap ₹95,000 for 30 dishes.

prompts.title: Liquor License Compliance Daybook for Bar
prompts.prompt: Act as an excise-compliance consultant with 12 years of advising Indian restaurant bars in Maharashtra and Karnataka. Draft a daily liquor compliance daybook for my casual-dining restobar in Lower Parel, Mumbai (FL-III license under Bombay Prohibition Act). Include: (1) opening reconciliation (bottle count + permit register update), (2) dry-day check (mark on calendar — election, religious holidays), (3) RTGS purchase entry from authorized excise depot only, (4) on-shift checks (no service to under-25 / no service to visibly intoxicated), (5) ID verification SOP (only original Aadhaar/Passport/DL accepted; not e-copies), (6) breakage and spoilage register, (7) closing reconciliation, (8) monthly excise return data points (Form X). Format as a single-page daybook with checkbox + sign-off rows. Constraints: align with Maharashtra Excise rules; flag the 25-year minimum age and the FL-III hours (typical 11:00-1:30 AM); training language Hinglish-friendly for bar captain.

prompts.title: Pre-Opening Soft Launch Plan for New Outlet
prompts.prompt: Act as a restaurant pre-opening consultant with 16 years across Indian launches. Build me a 21-day soft-launch plan for my new 80-cover Awadhi restaurant in Koramangala 5th Block, Bengaluru. Cover: Day -21 to -1 stage-by-stage with (1) team hiring + training calendar, (2) menu engineering + food cost validation, (3) supplier onboarding (mandis, dairy, meat, beverage), (4) FSSAI + trade + signage license tracking, (5) tech stack go-live (POS like PetPooja, KDS, IVR, Swiggy/Zomato/Magicpin onboarding), (6) marketing (Insta seeding, hyperlocal influencers ₹3-15K range, Google My Business, Magicpin gold), (7) friends-and-family soft-launch nights (60 covers x 3 nights, with feedback form), (8) public launch readiness gate (hit 4.0+ Zomato pre-rating). Format as a Gantt-style table. Constraints: stay within ₹14L pre-launch marketing budget; prioritise organic over paid for first 30 days; document 'no-Insta-press' policy for soft launch.

prompts.title: Dietary Tag and Allergen Audit for Menu
prompts.prompt: Act as an FSSAI menu-labeling consultant with 10 years of compliance work. Audit my current 60-item menu for allergen and dietary tags. I will paste the menu. For each item return: (1) FSSAI allergen tag (gluten, dairy, egg, nut, soy, sesame, mustard, fish, shellfish), (2) dietary tag (veg, non-veg, eggetarian, vegan, jain, gluten-free, low-sugar), (3) any item where the menu currently mis-tags (false-veg, hidden dairy via ghee/curd, gelatine in dessert), (4) suggested menu copy revision, (5) printable allergen icon legend. Format as a 5-column audit table + a remediation summary. Constraints: align with FSSAI 2020 Labelling and Display Regulations and the 2024 menu disclosure update; jain rules cover no onion/garlic/root; allergen statement must be visible on menu, aggregator app, and POS receipt.

prompts.title: Loyalty Program Design for Restaurant Chain
prompts.prompt: Act as a loyalty program designer with 8 years across Indian F&B brands (similar to EazyDiner Prime, Pizza Hut Hut Rewards). Design a tiered loyalty program for my 6-outlet North Indian chain in Pune. Target: lift repeat rate from 22% to 38% in 6 months. Provide: (1) 3 tiers (Silver / Gold / Platinum) with INR spend thresholds + benefit ladder (e.g., 10% / 15% / 20% off, free dessert, birthday treat, valet, priority Zomato), (2) point earn + redemption math (1 point per ₹10, ₹1 per point, 60% redemption rate assumption), (3) program economics (average impact on margin per tier, payback period), (4) WhatsApp + app onboarding flow (Razorpay-linked PG), (5) dunning + lapsed-member win-back triggers, (6) DPDP-compliant consent and data minimization. Format as a program brief + 12-month financial projection. Constraints: avoid dependency on third-party loyalty (own first-party data); DPDP 2023-ready consent flow; staff incentive aligned to Platinum-member acquisition.

prompts.title: Daily Operational Dashboard for Owner
prompts.prompt: Act as a restaurant operations analyst with 9 years of building owner dashboards for Indian QSR and casual-dine. Design a single-page daily ops dashboard my Founder reads at 11 AM IST every morning. Pull data from PetPooja POS export and Swiggy/Zomato dashboards. Include: (1) yesterday's revenue (dine-in + delivery + take-away) vs same-day-last-week, (2) covers + average ticket, (3) top 5 selling dishes + bottom 5 (with cost % flag), (4) Swiggy/Zomato rating trail (rolling 7 day) + open complaints, (5) inventory red-zone (items below reorder), (6) labour productivity (revenue per server hour), (7) one-line auto-narrative summarising 'what changed today'. Format as a 1-page printable PDF mock-up + a 2-line CSV row for archiving. Constraints: dashboard must load within 8 seconds on a 4G connection; tolerate Swiggy/Zomato API outages; INR all-inclusive of GST.

prompts.title: Regional Cuisine Specials Calendar (12 Months)
prompts.prompt: Act as a culinary curator with 12 years of festival and regional cuisine programming for Indian restaurants. Build me a 12-month regional cuisine specials calendar for my multi-cuisine restaurant in Hyderabad. Each month features one Indian region with a 5-dish food festival (e.g., February-Bengali, April-Tamil, June-Kashmiri, August-Awadhi, October-Bohra, December-Goan). For each month deliver: (1) region + theme name, (2) 5-dish menu with descriptions, (3) sourcing brief for region-authentic ingredients (and Hyderabad-substitute if not available), (4) 2-week marketing build-up (Insta carousel + reel + WhatsApp blast), (5) influencer seed list (3 names per region for Hyderabad reach), (6) decor + music mood, (7) staff training one-pager. Format as a 12-row calendar. Constraints: avoid clashing with major North-Indian festival weeks (Diwali, Holi); cap food cost at 34%; align launch dates to a Tuesday for soft trial.

prompts.title: Vendor Negotiation Email for Mandi Pricing
prompts.prompt: Act as a procurement manager with 8 years of negotiating with Indian wholesale food vendors. Draft a 200-word email to my onion-tomato vendor at Vashi APMC, Navi Mumbai requesting a 12-month price-protection contract. I run a 4-outlet biryani chain consuming 1,200 kg of onions and 600 kg of tomatoes weekly. Email should: (1) acknowledge his 6-year supply relationship and loyalty bonus, (2) propose a transparent index-linked pricing model (NHRDF wholesale ± 5%), (3) define quality grade specs (size, firmness, moisture), (4) cap weekly supply at 1.4x average to absorb seasonal spikes, (5) include a fortnightly review clause and a force-majeure clause (lockdown, mandi strike), (6) request signed contract within 14 days. Tone: warm and trust-building, not adversarial. Format as a ready-to-send email with subject + signature. Constraints: respect 'mandi handshake' culture; avoid lawyerly language in body but include 1-line legal-ready 'subject to written contract' clause; mention GST invoice requirement.

prompts.title: Beverage Menu Engineering with Mocktails
prompts.prompt: Act as a beverage program manager with 10 years across Indian fine-dining and lounges. Build me a beverage menu for my 100-cover restaurant in Vasant Kunj, Delhi (no liquor license — alcohol-free). Include: (1) 6 signature mocktails using Indian botanicals (kokum, bel, gulkand, jamun, hibiscus, kala namak), (2) 4 cold-brew + filter coffee options (Blue Tokai or local Yelagiri sourcing), (3) 5 traditional beverages (kadhi-chai, aam panna, jaljeera, masala milk, sol kadi), (4) 4 mineral / kombucha / health-shot options (priced premium), (5) for each item: glassware, garnish, recipe (ml level), batch prep notes, plate cost, suggested menu price. Format as a printable menu sheet + costing summary. Constraints: target 18-22% beverage cost; mocktail prices in ₹220-₹450 band; signature drink names should be Sanskrit-Hindi evocative; minimum 3 zero-sugar options.

prompts.title: Wastage Reduction Program for Kitchen
prompts.prompt: Act as a sustainability and ops consultant with 8 years auditing Indian restaurant wastage. Design a 90-day wastage reduction program for my 3-outlet restaurant chain. Current wastage: 9% of revenue (industry benchmark in India is 4-6%). Cover: (1) baseline wastage audit (week 1) — by category (raw, prep, plate-return, expiry), (2) root cause analysis (over-portioning, par stock, prep forecast errors, supplier yield), (3) 6 interventions (portion-control scoops, dual-use ingredient cards, half-portion menu, prep cycle change, FIFO labels, BBE auto-alerts), (4) tech enablement (PetPooja waste log + supplier yield app), (5) staff incentive (1% wastage drop = 0.25% of saving paid as bonus), (6) measurement KPIs (₹/cover wastage, week-on-week trend), (7) compostable food waste tie-up (BBMP-listed vendor for Bengaluru / SDMC for Delhi). Format as a 90-day Gantt + KPI dashboard mock-up. Constraints: zero capex above ₹40,000; no menu price hike; compliant with Solid Waste Mgmt Rules 2016.

prompts.title: Catering Quote for Indian Wedding
prompts.prompt: Act as a catering business manager with 12 years of pricing Indian wedding contracts. Build a catering quote for a 350-pax sangeet dinner in Jaipur. Client brief: 5 PM-11 PM, 6 live counters, vegetarian + select non-veg, 3 cocktail mocktails, dessert station, family-style mithai counter, dedicated jain chef. Provide: (1) menu with 28 items across counters + buffet, (2) per-pax cost (raw + labour + setup + transport + service charge), (3) suggested quote price with 30% catering margin, (4) staffing plan (chefs, helpers, captains, runners — INR daily rates), (5) equipment hire (chafing dishes, live grills, induction, glassware — local Jaipur vendor), (6) timeline from advance to delivery (7-day countdown), (7) payment milestones (40% advance, 30% T-7, 30% T+1), (8) risk register (rain, power-cut, dietary surprise). Format as a signed-quote PDF outline. Constraints: GST 5% on outdoor catering; FSSAI + temporary food license check; allergen disclosure to host family in writing.

prompts.title: Staff Scheduling for Peak Friday-Sunday
prompts.prompt: Act as a restaurant ops manager with 9 years of staff rostering across Indian dine-in chains. Build a Friday-to-Sunday staff schedule for my 80-cover restaurant in Andheri West, Mumbai. Cover three shifts (lunch 11-3, snack 3-7, dinner 7-12) with the following positions: 1 manager, 2 captains, 6 waiters, 1 hostess, 4 kitchen line cooks, 2 helpers, 1 cleaner. Also factor: weekend covers projected at 1.8x weekday, 12% staff sick-leave buffer, ₹120/hour overtime above 9 hours per shift, mandatory 1 weekly off rotated. Provide: (1) 3-day shift roster table, (2) cover-to-staff ratio per shift, (3) overtime cost projection, (4) call-list for short-notice replacements, (5) staff meal schedule, (6) handover checklist between shifts. Format as a printable PDF roster. Constraints: align with Shops & Establishments Act for Maharashtra (max 9 hours/day, 48/week); avoid back-to-back close-then-open shifts; one female-friendly closing shift policy.

prompts.title: Customer Feedback Form (QR-Based)
prompts.prompt: Act as a CX program designer with 7 years of restaurant feedback systems. Design a QR-code-driven customer feedback form for my casual-dining outlet in Indiranagar, Bengaluru. Goals: capture honest diner feedback before they post on Zomato, drive private resolution, and feed weekly leadership review. Provide: (1) the 10 feedback questions (mix of NPS, dish-level rating, service rating, ambience, value, return likelihood, free-text comment, photo upload, contact for follow-up, consent), (2) the QR placement plan (table tent, bill folder, exit signage), (3) the post-submission flow (auto-thank-you, manager Slack alert if NPS<7, GMB review nudge if NPS>=9), (4) weekly leadership digest format, (5) DPDP-compliant consent and data deletion policy, (6) integration suggestion (Google Form -> Slack -> Sheets, or paid tools like Reelo / RestrooNow). Format as a UX flow + sample form copy. Constraints: form completion under 90 seconds; mobile-first; keep one Hindi toggle option.

prompts.title: Influencer Outreach Email for Restaurant
prompts.prompt: Act as a hyperlocal marketing manager with 6 years of restaurant influencer outreach in Indian metros. Draft an outreach email + Instagram DM template to invite 12 micro food-creators (5K-50K followers) to my new restaurant launch in HSR Layout, Bengaluru. Restaurant: 70-cover Modern Indian, average ticket ₹900. Email should: (1) personalize using a recent post they made, (2) explain the launch concept in 2 lines (no marketing fluff), (3) offer the visit terms (full meal for 2 + ₹2,000 transport reimbursement), (4) ask for 1 reel + 2 stories with specific deliverable timelines, (5) clarify ASCI-mandated #ad disclosure, (6) propose 3 visit slots in the next 14 days, (7) close with a warm sign-off and direct WhatsApp number. Also draft a 60-word Instagram DM version. Format as two ready-to-send messages. Constraints: avoid generic 'we love your work'; offer must be honest, not exploitative; avoid cash payouts; legal — comply with ASCI Influencer Guidelines 2021.

prompts.title: POS Data SQL Queries for Daily Insights
prompts.prompt: Act as a data analyst with 5 years of working with PetPooja and Posist POS exports for Indian restaurants. Write 8 SQL queries (BigQuery / Postgres flavour) on my POS data tables — orders(order_id, outlet, channel, ts, total, gst, discount, tip), order_items(order_id, dish_id, qty, price, cost), dishes(dish_id, name, category, recipe_cost). Queries: (1) yesterday top 10 dishes by gross revenue per outlet, (2) week-on-week dish revenue change, (3) average ticket by channel (dine-in / Swiggy / Zomato / direct), (4) lost-revenue from voided orders, (5) discount ratio % per outlet, (6) hour-of-day demand heatmap, (7) margin-eroding dishes (cost/price ratio > 35%), (8) repeat-customer share (using phone hash). Format each as labelled SQL block + a one-line business-decision tied to it. Constraints: assume IST timestamps; handle nulls; avoid SELECT *; keep query cost under ₹2 per run.

prompts.title: P&L Variance Commentary for Owner Review
prompts.prompt: Act as a finance partner to a restaurant founder, with 10 years analyzing Indian F&B P&Ls. Write the monthly P&L variance commentary for my Pune restaurant's October performance. I will paste a 2-month P&L (October vs September) showing: revenue, food cost, beverage cost, labour, rent, electricity, marketing, R&M, license fee, packaging, depreciation, EBITDA. Commentary should explain: (1) revenue variance (mix of channel, ticket, footfall), (2) cost variance with quantification (e.g., '₹1.4L higher food cost driven by tomato spike + over-portioning at outlet B'), (3) one-time vs recurring items, (4) leading indicators for November (festive demand, new menu impact), (5) 3 Founder-actionable items with named owners and due dates, (6) red-flag risks (vendor over-concentration, single-channel dependence). Format as a 1-page memo with sections + a 5-row variance bridge table. Constraints: factor 18% GST on revenue; use ₹ Lakhs; tone of an internal CFO update, not generic.
